Video.summary: Steve discusses mindfulness meditation and how those who practice it reduce their stress, increase their contentment, and achieve more
Video.title: How To Find Inner Peace with Steve Taylor

Video.summary: What if the key to a vibrant, healthy life lies in returning to our ancestral roots? In this episode of Rooted in Wellness, I’m joined by Dr. Darshan Shah who works to merge ancient healing practices with modern-day science to help his patients extend both their lifespan and more importantly, their healthspan.
Video.title: Dr. Darshan Shah: The Most Important Habits to Prevent an Early Death from a Longevity Expert

Image.alt: Dr. Darshan Shah: The Most Important Habits to Prevent an Early Death from a Longevity Expert
Dr. Darshan Shah: The Most Important Habits to Prevent an Early Death from a Longevity Expert
Dr. Darshan Shah: The Most Important Habits to Prevent an Early Death from a Longevity Expert

Video.summary: Between work, family, and wellness, many women feel like they're running on empty. Mona shares empowering tools and mindset shifts to help women balance it all—without burning out. Fulfillment is possible when you root into what matters.
Video.title: Rooted In Wellness - FC Lisa Odenweller EP 10

Image.alt: Rooted In Wellness - FC Lisa Odenweller EP 10
Rooted In Wellness - FC Lisa Odenweller EP 10
Rooted In Wellness - FC Lisa Odenweller EP 10

Video.summary: Did you know a large part of your immune system resides in your gut? Our gut microbiome plays a huge role in our overall health but oftentimes have been overlooked as nothing but a digestive workhorse.
Video.title: Hannah Kleinfeld: Gut Health and the Microbiome: Improving and Maintaining the Microbiome with Probiotics + More

Image.alt: Hannah Kleinfeld: Gut Health and the Microbiome: Improving and Maintaining the Microbiome with Probiotics + More
Hannah Kleinfeld: Gut Health and the Microbiome: Improving and Maintaining the Microbiome with Probiotics + More
Hannah Kleinfeld: Gut Health and the Microbiome: Improving and Maintaining the Microbiome with Probiotics + More

Video.summary: Today Lisa speaks with Udo Erasmus, best-selling author of “Fats that Heal, Fats that Kill.” As an expert in health and healing, he talks about “good fats,” healthy eating, and ways to reconnect to the self and live a life of thriving! Info: Udoschoice.com.
Video.title: The Aware Show - Healthy Fats that Heal

Image.alt: The Aware Show - Healthy Fats that Heal
The Aware Show - Healthy Fats that Heal
The Aware Show - Healthy Fats that Heal

Video.summary: Play To Win is BACK!... and the competition is tougher than ever. Don't miss out as leaders from around the world go head to head to win a spot on the Higdon Group team.
Video.title: Play To Win Season 2- Episode 1

Video.summary: Here is Episode 2 of Play to Win! And the stories get even more powerful with these new contestants. Who are you rooting for to win?!
Video.title: Play To Win Season 2- Episode 2

Video.summary: Jeremiah Ramirez, founder of The Marathon Group, seeks $750,000 to propel his financial brokerage dedicated to promoting financial literacy and insurance sales. Despite Ramirez's personal drive stemming from past hardships, the panelists question the feasibility of his plan, particularly his proposal to cover agents' continuing education costs.
Video.title: Fund Tank - The Marathon Group

Video.summary: Grief is the death of something—someone, a relationship, a job, or a dream. There’s no timeline, and you cannot heal what you do not feel. Guided by David Kessler, this course helps you navigate your river of grief, honor your unique process, and find a path toward healing.
Video.grandparentTitle: Help for the Hurting Heart with David Kessler
Video.title: Help for the Hurting Heart Day 1

Image.alt: Help for the Hurting Heart Day 1
Help for the Hurting Heart Day 1
Help for the Hurting Heart Day 1

Video.summary: Grief is about the heart, but the mind shapes the story we tell. While you can’t change the facts of loss, you can change the narrative, honor old wounds, and choose words that guide you toward growth, meaning, and deeper connection to joy and healing.
Video.grandparentTitle: Help for the Hurting Heart with David Kessler
Video.title: Help for the Hurting Heart Day 2

Image.alt: Help for the Hurting Heart Day 2
Help for the Hurting Heart Day 2
Help for the Hurting Heart Day 2

Video.summary: Day 1 welcomes you to Desire Mapping—an approach to living guided by how you want to feel, not just what you want to do. By shifting from goal lists to heart-centered intention, this introduction invites you to honor presence, intuition, and the feelings that shape a fulfilling life.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Desire Map Course with Danielle LaPorte
Video.title: Part 1, Day 1 - Welcome

Video.summary: Desire Mapping guides you to live from the heart, focusing on how you want to feel rather than just what you want to do. Through practices like Soul Limber, appreciation, attention management, Core Desired Feelings, and exploring your inner selves, you gain clarity, agency, joy, and purpose in creating a fulfilling life.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Desire Map Course with Danielle LaPorte
Video.title: Part 1, Day 2-7

Video.summary: Daymara had big plans in 2020. Trips, work goals, and family outings were a few items on Daymara's to-do list. But when she collapsed getting out of bed one morning, all those plans on the horizon were immediately put on hold. After being rushed to the hospital Daymara was faced with an unexpected diagnosis. She had acute myeloid leukemia, a type of blood cancer. As this type of Leukemia can be aggressive, Daymara immediately began an intensive chemotherapy regime and a groundbreaking stem cell procedure that would eventually save her life. This stem cell procedure however would force her into isolation as her immune system was weakened. Regardless of what may have seemed like an insurmountable challenge, Daymara persevered and her determination to leave the hospital so she could be with her children again is what Daymara says gave her hope and the ability to get well again.
Video.title: Hope Is Here S2E1

Video.summary: Early on in Rachel Collin's pregnancy, she was told there was a concern for her unborn child as doctors discovered her baby's heart was undeveloped. Soon hopes were dashed and worry set in as Rachel was told the future was uncertain for her baby boy. Hope is Here chronicles the first two years of Mikey Collins' life, who proved to be a brave little boy with an incredible will to live.
Video.title: Hope Is Here S2E2

Video.summary: Five decades ago, Lawrence Monk Terry was at the top of his game. In 1972 he won a silver medal with the U.S. rowing team at the 1972 Summer Olympics in Munich Germany. His position on the team was an important one. He served as the Stroke. That's the term for the one who sets the pace for the rest of the rowers. Since then, his life has been rowing along merrily. Just recently he was a fit and active retiree until an April day, after Monk was doing housework and decided it was time to come inside that his life would take a pause. What happened next would give a whole new meaning to his revered rowing position, The Stroke.
Video.title: Hope Is Here S2E3

Video.summary: Your Day 1 practice offers an invitation to slow down and take stock.
Video.title: Yoga For All Day 1: Notice

Video.summary: Your Day 2 session invites you to use the tools of the at-home yoga practice to befriend your body and your breath. Build on the art of noticing from Day 1 as we expand our vocabulary with a focus on a strong foundation. This session encourages a healthy flow of energy up and down the spine and lovingly guides you to learn how to take responsibility for your experience on the mat.
Video.title: Day 2: Befriend

Video.summary: Your day 3 practice invites you to get to know your body through the structure of the practice and the art of aligning. We build on day 1 and 2 with a focus on action and alignment through asana and energy.
Video.title: Day 3: Align

Video.summary: Your Day 4 practice provides you the opportunity to lean in when you feel discomfort to embrace the process of finding ease. Discover layers of your home practice as we look at our patterns and continue to build a dynamic vocabulary that serves.
Video.title: Day 4: Ease

Video.summary: Founder, Glenn Marsden, of Imperfectly Perfect Campaign, and Jeremy Jackson, former Baywatch star share their personal journeys with body dysmorphia and how it led to a worldwide campaign. A young man faces his fears and a tale of kindness at 30,000 ft.
Video.title: Good Morning Joy - Imperfectly Perfect

Video.summary: Laura Di Franco, CEO of Brave Healer Productions, discusses brave healing and the power of sharing our stories. And, meet Oyen the ginger cat and adorable kids from India, along with Daaji redefining meditation for a joyful heart.
Video.title: Good Morning Joy - Steps to Brave Healing

Image.alt: Good Morning Joy - Steps to Brave Healing
Good Morning Joy - Steps to Brave Healing
Good Morning Joy - Steps to Brave Healing

Video.summary: In the following lesson, we'll discover how the ego mind influences both our way of thinking and our relationships. We'll also identify the unconscious barriers we've built that cause us to form these judgments and thoughts. That's where the problem is, and that's where the solution lies.
Video.title: Relationships: Overcoming the Ego Mind
